mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances when the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 55% over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which triggers short-term positive market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of periods following a 1% monthly increase in industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months where CPI exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding economic output, which directly supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of periods following the trigger activation.
